original_abstract:
Data scarcity remains a formidable obstacle in the development of deep learning (DL) models, which traditionally require vast datasets to achieve high performance. In many specialized fields, obtaining sufficient labeled data is difficult because the annotation process necessitates expert knowledge and is inherently costly, time-consuming, and prone to human error. This dependency on large-scale datasets often discourages the adoption of DL in domains where data is limited. Ensuring data sufficiency and quality is the foundational step toward building successful and reliable DL applications. 

This paper provides a holistic survey of state-of-the-art techniques designed to train DL models under constrained conditions, specifically targeting three core challenges: small datasets, class imbalance, and poor generalization. The review begins by categorizing fundamental learning techniques and DL architectures. It then provides an in-depth analysis of modern solutions for data scarcity, including Transfer Learning (TL), Self-Supervised Learning (SSL), Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), specialized Model Architectures (MA), Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINN), and Deep Synthetic Minority Oversampling Techniques (DeepSMOTE). Furthermore, the paper offers practical strategies for data acquisition and recommendations for ensuring the trustworthiness of training sets. Finally, we examine various applications currently hampered by data scarcity—ranging from Medical Imaging and Fluid Mechanics to Cybersecurity and Wireless Communications—and propose specific data-generation alternatives for each. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first review to offer such an exhaustive overview of strategies to mitigate data scarcity in deep learning.
humanized_abstract:
Data scarcity is a major challenge faced in developing a deep learning (DL) model. A deep learning model requires a large amount of data to learn efficiently. A large number of fields face the issue of insufficient labeled data. The reason being, labeling data requires deep expertise. It is very expensive, highly time-consuming and it involves human errors. Due to this factor, many fields refrain from using DL as they do not have sufficient data to meet the needs of the model. Ensuring data sufficiency and quality is the very first step towards building a good and efficient DL application.

Training Deep Learning models under limited resources is highly demanded due to its numerous benefits. As a result, several methods and solutions have been proposed to resolve the challenges that Deep Learning (DL) models face. The present paper proposes a comprehensive survey of deep learning methods and solutions to train the Deep Learning models efficiently while resolving four essential challenges such as limited resources, class imbalance, small datasets and poor model performance. The survey includes a comprehensive introduction of the Deep Learning fundamentals and architectures. Furthermore, an in-depth survey of recent and novel methods proposed for efficient deep learning model training in the presence of data scarcity is provided. The various proposed solutions in the literature namely Transfer Learning (TL), Self-Supervised Learning (SSL), Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Model Architectures (MA), Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINN) and Deep Synthetic Minority Oversampling Techniques (DeepSMOTE) are discussed. Besides, several techniques for data acquisition and training data validation are discussed. Lastly, the applications that are suffering due to data scarcity are shown, including Medical Imaging, Fluid Mechanics, Cybersecurity and Wireless Communications along with possible alternatives for data-generation. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first review of the state-of-the-art techniques and methods proposed in the literature to tackle the effect of data scarcity in the deep learning models.
